name: Battery Capacity Converter (mAh to Wh)
description: Convert battery capacity between milliamp-hours (mAh) and watt-hours (Wh).
formulaDescription: Watt-hours measure total energy, whereas milliamp-hours measure charge. Voltage is the link between them.

example.explanation: A 5000mAh battery at 3.7V has 5000 * 3.7 / 1000 = 18.5 Wh of energy.

faqs.question: Why is Wh better than mAh for comparison?
faqs.answer: mAh doesn't account for voltage. A 5000mAh 3.7V battery has much less energy than a 5000mAh 11.1V battery. Wh represents the actual work the battery can do.

faqs.question: What is the standard voltage for a LiPo cell?
faqs.answer: Nominal voltage is 3.7V per cell. Fully charged is 4.2V.

concept: Understanding battery capacity in Wh is crucial for air travel regulations and drone flight endurance calculations.
guideSteps: Enter the capacity value you want to convert.
Select whether the input is in mAh or Wh.
Enter the nominal voltage of the battery.
The calculator will show the equivalent value in the other unit.
standards: Airline Limit: 100Wh (usually the limit for carry-on without approval)
